question: Mark–Matthew
conclusion: Matthew most likely used Mark or a Mark-like written narrative source.

question: Mark–Luke (direct)
conclusion: Luke probably used Mark or a Mark-like written narrative source; requires order-retention penalty against shared-tradition models.
confidence: Medium-high vs Mark-used-Luke; medium against all alternatives

question: Matthew–Luke double tradition
conclusion: The non-Markan double tradition is better modeled as a shared sayings/tradition layer than as simple one-way direct copying.

question: System-level model family
conclusion: Markan priority plus a non-Markan sayings/tradition layer is the best-supported system family; Two-source and Farrer remain the two central scholarly competitors.

question: John–Synoptics
conclusion: John shares substantial anchor traditions with the Synoptics; shared anchor/passion/sign tradition is currently lower burden than direct Gospel-to-Gospel dependence. Local direct use remains possible anchor by anchor.

question: Minor agreements
conclusion: Minor agreements are a central unresolved discriminator; 118 algorithmic rows (21 high-strength) now constrain the Two-source / Farrer / Griesbach debate. Catalog requires hand curation before final claims.

question: Thomas–Synoptics
conclusion: Thomas is a curated logion-level sayings-network witness; 116 logia, 46 curated parallels, only 3 directional-claim-ready. Coptic–Greek automatic dependence not scored.
confidence: Medium for overlap; low for direction

question: 1 Timothy 5:18 / Luke 10:7
conclusion: One of the strongest cases for a Luke-like written Jesus saying; not necessarily canonical Luke in final form.

question: James / Gospel sayings
conclusion: James likely reflects Jesus tradition; direct use of Matthew or Luke is not demonstrated.

question: Paul/Luke eucharistic overlap
conclusion: 1 Cor 11 / Luke 22 is best read as shared liturgical/formula tradition, not direct Pauline dependence on Luke.

question: Q as a discrete document
conclusion: Supports a shared sayings/tradition layer; does not by itself prove one discrete recoverable Q document.
confidence: Medium-high for shared non-Markan sayings; medium-low for a discrete Q document

question: Other apocrypha beyond Thomas
conclusion: Inventory/roadmap only; no firm dependence conclusions yet. Gospel of Peter and passion apocrypha are highest-value next test cases.
